recalls.Description: The North Face is recalling its Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots, available in sizes 5 to 11. These boots feature traditional lace-up designs and the brand's logo embossed on the side and printed on the insole. They were sold in several colors including white (style number NF0A8ADB3IE), gray (NF0A8ADB5IF), tan (NF0A8ADBHFQ), and black (NF0A8ADBKT0), with the style number located on the tongue label of the boots.
recalls.Title: The North Face Recalls Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled boots and reach out to The North Face at 800 297-9150, available from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall_wshellistaV@thenorthface.com or visit https://www.thenorthface.com/en-us/help/product-recall for further details. Additional information can be found by clicking on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of thenorthface.com.

Products.Name: The North Face Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,200 (In addition, about 5,900 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled North Face Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots in white, gray, tan and black
Location of style color code on the recalled North Face Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boot

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 28 reports of the top hook catching on the lace of the other boot, including 15 reports of minor injuries in the United States.

Retailers.Name: The North Face stores, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nordstrom, REI, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at thenorthface.com and other websites, from September 2024 through January 2025 for approximately $165.

Importers.Name: The North Face, a division of VF Outdoor LLC, of Denver, Colorado

Hazards.Name: The hook of one boot may catch on the lace of the other boot, creating a fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled boots and contact The North Face for instructions on returning them for a full refund, gift card, or check if purchased with cash. After reaching out, consumers will receive a prepaid shipping label to return the boots at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: The North Face recalls Women's Shellista V Mid Waterproof Boots due to a fall hazard. Stop using them and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: OdorStop is recalling its Boot and Shoe Dryers and Deodorizers, specifically model numbers OSOBSDD2 and OSOBSDD. These devices, designed to dry and deodorize footwear using high-output fans and a 3-hour timer, have a label on the bottom indicating the model and brand. The recall is prompted by a potential fire hazard due to a short circuit in the heat or ozone on-off switch, which may cause arcing.
recalls.Title: OdorStop Boot and Shoe Dryers and Deodorizers Recalled Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled products immediately and reach out to OdorStop LLC at 800-414-2191, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. For further assistance, email support@odorstop.com or visit www.OdorStop.com/recall for detailed information.

Products.Name: OdorStop Boot and Shoe Dryer Model OSOBSDD2

Images.Caption: Recalled OdorStop Boot and Shoe Dryers and Deodorizers, models OSBSDD2 & OSBSDD

Retailers.Name: Available online through Amazon.com, Odorstop.com, Lowes.com, Globalindustrial.com, and webrestaurantstore.com from December 2018 to October 2023, priced between $100 and $130.

Importers.Name: OdorStop LLC, of Hamburg, New York

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to short circuiting in heat or ozone switch

Remedies.Name: Free repair by installing a new in-line fuse

recalls.metaDescription: OdorStop recalls Boot and Shoe Dryers due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact for a free repair. Learn more here.

recalls.Description: Boot Royalty has announced a recall of the Men's Dark Brown Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boots, model SE4570, manufactured in July and August 2022. These slip-on leather boots resemble cowboy boots and feature a metatarsal guard and steel toe for foot protection. Available in men's sizes 7 to 14, the boots are tan and brown with decorative stitching. A red label on the outsole displays the name "Justin," which is also embossed on the leather near the heel. The products include labels indicating "Steel Toe" and "Met Guard" on the side seams, and a heat-transferred label inside the shaft shows the manufacturing date code of 07/22 NH or 08/22 NH.
recalls.Title: Boot Royalty Issues Recall for Men's Leather Work Boots Due to Potential Crush Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers should reach out to Boot Royalty Company at 800-550-7327 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email customerservice@justinbrands.com. More information is available online at https://www.justinboots.com/ by clicking on the "Safety Recall" tab or visiting www.justinboots.com/safetyrecall.

Products.Name: Justin Boots Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boots

Images.Caption: Recalled Justin's Boots Keaven WP Met Guard Work Boot
Recalled boots manufacture date code label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of an injury when a heavy object fell on a consumer's foot, resulting in bruising.

Retailers.Name: Available at Grainger, Safety Shoes Distributors, Saf-Gard Safety Shoe Company, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.grainger.com and www.justinboots.com from September 2022 through September 2023, priced between $150 and $200.

Importers.Name: Boot Royalty Company L.P., of Fort Worth, Texas

Hazards.Name: Potential absence of metatarsal guard leading to impact risk for users

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled boots and contact Boot Royalty Company for instructions on obtaining free replacement boots.

recalls.metaDescription: Boot Royalty recalls Men's Leather Work Boots due to crush hazard. Contact for free replacements.

recalls.Description: H.H. Brown Shoe Company has announced a recall of the Redeemer DH 5379 Men's 11" Waterproof Metguard Comp Toe Ropers Work Boots due to a potential injury risk. These pull-on boots feature a brown leather upper with a western-style design, square toe, one-inch heel, EVA midsole, and metatarsal guard. The boots include embossed labels indicating 'Phantom Rider,' 'Non-Metallic Toe,' and 'Puncture Resisting Sole,' along with a black label stating 'Waterproof.' Only boots with manufacturing codes VCJJ22, VCJC23, VCJD23, and VCJF23 are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: H.H. Brown Shoe Company Issues Recall for Redeemer DH 5379 Work Boots Due to Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled boots immediately and reach out to H.H. Brown Shoe Company at 800-441-4319 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email DH5379recall@hhbrown.com or visit https://www.doublehboots.com/safetyrecall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Redeemer DH 5379 Men's 11" Waterproof Metguard Comp Toe Roper Work Boot
Inside label with model code and manufacturing code

Retailers.Name: Available at workwear and shoe retailers nationwide and online at doublehboots.com from February 2023 to October 2023, priced around $190.

Importers.Name: H.H. Brown Shoe Company Inc., of Greenwich, Connecticut

Distributors.Name: H.H. Brown Shoe Company Inc., of Greenwich, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Potential injury due to non-puncture-resistant sole.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the boots and contact H.H. Brown for a prepaid shipping label to return them for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: H.H. Brown recalls Redeemer DH 5379 Work Boots due to injury risks. Contact them for a replacement if you own these boots.
